_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Biliary or Pancreatic ductal system disease treatment or tissue sampling
Biliary stenting for strictures and leakage
Balloon dilation of duodenal papilla and ductal strictures

preauth.label: Clinical Notes with history and examination with indication for the procedure

claim.label: Invoice or barcode of the stent (if used)

ppd_preauth.q: Admission notes comprising of history and examination with indications for the procedure?
Are the relevant investigations like CBC, LFT and USG establishing diagnosis submitted?
Were the patient's clinical history/investigations indicative of the Procedure?
Whether the investigation reports confirm the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details available?
Are the detailed Operative/Procedure notes available?
Is the discharge summary with follow-up advice available at the time of discharge?
Is the invoice or barcode of the stent (if used) submitted?

common_queries: Clinical history/investigations are not indicative of the procedure.
Investigation reports do not confirm the diagnosis.
Mandatory clinical notes with history and examination are missing or incomplete at pre-authorisation.
Mandatory investigations (CBC, LFT, USG Abdomen) are missing at pre-authorisation.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) are missing or incomplete at claim submission.
Detailed Operative/Procedure notes are missing or incomplete at claim submission.
ERCP photograph is missing at claim submission.
Invoice or barcode of the stent (if used) is missing at claim submission.
Detailed Discharge Summary is missing or incomplete at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Endoscopic Retrograde Cholangiopancreatography (ERCP) is a combined endoscopic and fluoroscopic procedure in which an upper endoscope is led into a second part of the duodenum, making it possible for the passage of other tools via the major duodenal papilla into the biliary and pancreatic ducts.
ERCP as a Diagnostic Procedure: To perform Cholangiopancreatoscopy, biopsy, brush cytology or ductal ultrasound.
ERCP as a Therapeutic Procedure: To perform Sphincterotomy, stent placement and stone removal.
